Title: Molecular changes in agroinfiltrated leaves of Nicotiana benthamiana expressing suppressor of silencing P19 and coronavirus-like particles

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expression reduces CoVLP-induced defence gene expression and leaf necrosis without reducing S protein accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbKTI5 transcripts were basally present in non-infiltrated leaves but strongly repressed after agroinfiltration. 2. Transient co-expression of NbKTI5 with CoVLPs reduced leaf necrosis and curling. 3. NbKTI5 lowered expression of CoVLP-induced defence markers NbBBE2, NbPPO1/3, NbKTI3 and NbPDF1, while not significantly changing S protein levels.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; symptom scoring; Western blot/capillary immunoassay for S protein; RT-qPCR of defence marker genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Basal expression in NI leaves; strongly repressed shortly after agroinfiltration in both P19 and CoVLP samples.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; CoVLP expression; co-expression with helper KTI transgene
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Kunitz trypsin inhibitor family (protease inhibitor family I03)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No adverse effect on S protein accumulation was detected; broader effects on endogenous protease balance remain possible.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted 239-aa secreted glycoprotein with 24-aa signal peptide, vacuolar sorting signal NPIVL, conserved Kunitz motif, and putative N-glycosylation site. Proposed to maintain PI/CP balance and antagonize defence-associated PCD.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful helper protein for plant molecular farming to improve biomass quality during CoVLP production without compromising productivity.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expression reduces CoVLP-induced defence gene expression and leaf symptoms without affecting S protein accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbKTI7 showed basal expression in NI leaves but was strongly repressed after agroinfiltration. 2. Co-expression of NbKTI7 with CoVLPs reduced necrosis and leaf curling. 3. NbKTI7 decreased transcript abundance of CoVLP-induced defence genes while leaving S protein levels unchanged.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; symptom scoring; Western blot/capillary immunoassay for S protein; RT-qPCR of defence marker genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Basal expression in NI leaves; strongly repressed shortly after agroinfiltration in both P19 and CoVLP samples.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; CoVLP expression; co-expression with helper KTI transgene
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Kunitz trypsin inhibitor family (protease inhibitor family I03)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No adverse effect on S protein accumulation was detected; effects on general protease homeostasis may need evaluation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted 221-aa secreted glycoprotein with 30-aa signal peptide, conserved Kunitz motif, and three putative N-glycosylation sites; no vacuolar sorting signal identified. Proposed to compensate for loss of endogenous KTI activity during stress.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising helper protein for reducing defence and PCD during recombinant protein production in plants.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the plastid branch leading to salicylic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time-course expression profiling; inference from hormone-marker gene behavior
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a key regulatory node associated with early activation of salicylic acid signalling after agroinfiltration; expression declines upon S accumulation, consistent with dampened SA responses.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbICS2 was robustly induced from 2 to 5 DPI in P19 samples. 2. It was also induced at 2 DPI in CoVLP samples but decreased as S protein accumulated. 3. The authors propose NbICS2 as a key regulatory node controlling hormonal crosstalk during Agrobacterium-mediated foreign protein expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced early after agroinfiltration in leaves P9/P10; stronger and more sustained in P19 than in CoVLP at later stages.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; P19 expression; co-expression of SARS-CoV-2 S protein/CoVLP formation; 2–6 DPI time course
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in Discussion/Figure S10 rather than central Results validation; role inferred from transcriptional patterns and marker genes, not direct SA quantification in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to rebalance SA–oxylipin crosstalk during transient expression systems to optimize biomass health and recombinant protein production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts free salicylic acid into inactive conjugated glucosylated forms.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time-course expression profiling; proteomics at 6 DPI
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA inactivation and is associated with dampening of SA responses in CoVLP samples.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbSAGT1 was strongly induced in CoVLP samples. 2. The corresponding protein also accumulated at higher levels in CoVLP samples at 6 DPI. 3. These data support enhanced SA conjugation/inactivation during S protein expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in CoVLP-expressing leaves during late response.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; P19 expression; co-expression of SARS-CoV-2 S protein/CoVLP formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as mechanistic support for SA pathway dampening rather than primary functional target of manipulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to preserve SA homeostasis or control excessive defence during plant molecular farming.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; membrane-tethered UPR regulator
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: endoplasmic reticulum quality control and secretory protein folding capacity
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ERQC genes; ERAD genes; PDI; BiP; CNX; CRT; additional UPR-associated NAC genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates UPR-associated genes during S protein expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master switch for UPR activation that helps cells cope with secretory burden during recombinant S production and CoVLP assembly.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Early and transient induction marks activation of the UPR in CoVLP-expressing leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbbZIP60 was induced upon S protein expression with stronger upregulation in CoVLP than P19 samples. 2. Its expression peaked early, matching transient UPR activation. 3. The study interprets NbbZIP60 as a master switch coordinating ER stress responses during CoVLP production.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early induction with maximum around 3–4 DPI in CoVLP-expressing P9/P10 leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; transient expression of P19 and SARS-CoV-2 S protein; ER secretory stress
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ER stress caused by secreted recombinant S protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation reflects cellular stress burden during molecular farming.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient-metabolism regulator per se; included because it is a primary experimentally emphasized regulator connected to metabolic and secretory stress reprogramming.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Presumed activators of JA signalling outputs; all were severely repressed in P19 and CoVLP samples.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Considered master switches for activation of JA signalling in N. benthamiana.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Severe repression indicates canonical JA signalling is not the dominant driver of CoVLP-induced oxylipin responses.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbMYC2 homologues were all severely repressed in both P19 and CoVLP samples. 2. This suggests JA signalling was not strongly activated despite oxylipin-responsive gene induction. 3. The data support a model in which oxylipins other than JA contribute substantially to defence activation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly repressed after agroinfiltration in both P19 and CoVLP leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; P19 expression; S protein accumulation/CoVLP formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Hormonal crosstalk with SA and oxylipin pathways
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repression may constrain beneficial JA-dependent defence outputs while shifting signalling to alternative oxylipins.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped because the paper discusses them collectively as Arabidopsis MYC2 homologues with shared behaviour.
